summary: Gruber's 2019 Apple Report Card delivers harsh grades across most categories, with Mac software (Catalina) and iPadOS receiving the sharpest criticism. He praises Mac and iPad hardware but argues that software quality has dragged both platforms down significantly. iPhone hardware earns high marks for its camera improvements, while AirPods Pro earn the only A grade. He criticizes Apple TV's remote, calls iPadOS multitasking 'incoherent,' and condemns Apple for allowing Trump to use the Mac Pro factory as a campaign backdrop.
key_points: MacOS Catalina's overzealous security permissions mirror the exact Windows Vista behavior Apple once mocked, and the OS is abnormally buggy months after release
iPadOS multitasking is fundamentally unintuitive and incoherent — Gruber wishes he could run regular iOS on his iPad Pro instead
iPhone 11 cameras represent a stellar hardware year, and iOS 13 with Shortcuts is the most exciting power-user feature in iOS history
Apple's hardware reliability and AirPods Pro earned top marks, but software quality across Mac, iPad, and Apple TV dragged the overall picture down
Apple allowing Trump to use the Mac Pro assembly plant as a campaign backdrop was 'an absolute disgrace' that contradicts Apple's stated values
money_quotes: Back in 2007 Apple ran a 'Get a Mac' commercial mocking Windows Vista for this exact same sort of overzealous permission nagging. That's exactly what Catalina feels like.
Not one thing about Mac software got better in 2019 and everything that did change made it worse.
The iPadOS multitasking emperor has no clothes. I wish I could run iOS on my iPad Pro.
One of them is very cohesive, the other is incoherent.
If you want a second app next to the first one, you have to drag the icon for that app out of the Dock? Why in the world would dragging an app icon launch an instance of an app?
I've been using Mac laptops for 20 years and I've never once had an issue like that.
It's an absolute disgrace that Apple allowed Donald Trump to use the Mac Pro assembly plant in Austin as the backdrop for an event to promote his re-election.
I'll keep beating the drum that iCloud storage tiers are too small, though — especially the 5 GB free tier.
key_insight: Apple's 2019 hardware reached impressive heights across nearly every product line, but software quality — especially MacOS Catalina's security overreach and iPadOS's incoherent multitasking — has become a serious liability that undermines the hardware excellence.
